title: Listening - Part 1
description: You are going to hear people talking in eight situations. For questions 1–8, choose the best answer (A, B or C).
instructions: You will hear each piece twice. For questions 1–8, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear a man leaving a message for a friend.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Hi, Priya, it’s Nathan. I’ve booked seats for the comedy show tomorrow. It starts at eight, and the theatre asks us to be there twenty minutes before it begins. I was going to meet you outside the cinema first, but the café there has closed. ||Man: [1]Let’s meet by the ticket desk inside the theatre at half past seven instead[/1]. Text me if that’s a problem.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Hi, Priya, it’s Nathan. I’ve booked seats for the comedy show tomorrow. It starts at eight, and the theatre asks us to be there twenty minutes before it begins. I was going to meet you outside the cinema first, but the café there has closed.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Let’s meet by the ticket desk inside the theatre at half past seven instead. Text me if that’s a problem.

questions.question: Where does Nathan want to meet Priya?
questions.explanation: He changes the plan and asks Priya to meet by the theatre ticket desk (evidence [1]).

extracts.context: You hear two students talking about an online course.
extracts.audio_script: Girl: Did you finish that online drawing course? ||Boy: Yes. I liked the video lessons and it was useful being able to pause them. The weekly tasks were quite difficult, but that made me practise. ||Girl: Would you recommend it? ||Boy: Probably. [2]I just found it disappointing that nobody commented on my drawings, because I’d hoped for advice on how to improve[/2].

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Did you finish that online drawing course?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Yes. I liked the video lessons and it was useful being able to pause them. The weekly tasks were quite difficult, but that made me practise.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Would you recommend it?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Probably. I just found it disappointing that nobody commented on my drawings, because I’d hoped for advice on how to improve.

questions.question: What disappointed the boy about the course?

options.A: The exercises required too much practice.
options.B: The videos were difficult to follow.
options.C: He did not receive feedback on his work.

questions.explanation: He says nobody commented on his drawings, although he had wanted advice (evidence [2]).

extracts.context: You hear an announcement at an airport.
extracts.audio_script: Announcer: This is an announcement for passengers travelling on flight JR482 to Dublin. Boarding was expected to begin at Gate 16 in ten minutes. Due to a change of aircraft, the flight will now depart from another part of the terminal. ||Announcer: [3]Please make your way immediately to Gate 28, where boarding will begin shortly[/3]. Passengers needing assistance should speak to a member of staff.

dialogue.speaker: announcer
dialogue.text: This is an announcement for passengers travelling on flight JR482 to Dublin. Boarding was expected to begin at Gate 16 in ten minutes. Due to a change of aircraft, the flight will now depart from another part of the terminal.

dialogue.speaker: announcer
dialogue.text: Please make your way immediately to Gate 28, where boarding will begin shortly. Passengers needing assistance should speak to a member of staff.

questions.question: What are passengers travelling to Dublin asked to do?

options.A: board their flight at a different gate

questions.explanation: Passengers are told to go to Gate 28 instead of the original Gate 16 (evidence [3]).

extracts.context: You hear a radio interview with a woman who volunteers at an animal centre.
extracts.audio_script: Presenter: Why did you start volunteering at the animal centre, Kim? ||Woman: I love animals, but I live in a flat where pets aren’t allowed. At first, I imagined I’d spend all my time feeding dogs and cats. In fact, I clean cages and answer phones as well. [4]The most satisfying moment is when an animal leaves with a new owner and you know it will be cared for[/4].

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: Why did you start volunteering at the animal centre, Kim?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I love animals, but I live in a flat where pets aren’t allowed. At first, I imagined I’d spend all my time feeding dogs and cats. In fact, I clean cages and answer phones as well. The most satisfying moment is when an animal leaves with a new owner and you know it will be cared for.

questions.question: What does Kim find most rewarding about her volunteer work?

options.A: learning how to care for different pets
options.C: talking to people on the phone

questions.explanation: Kim says the most satisfying moment is when an animal goes home with a new owner (evidence [4]).

extracts.context: You hear a customer talking to a hairdresser.
extracts.audio_script: Hairdresser: How would you like your hair cut today? The same length as last time? ||Woman: I’m going on holiday somewhere hot, so I was thinking of having it much shorter. But I’m not sure that would suit me. ||Hairdresser: We could take a little off today and see how you feel. ||Woman: [5]Yes, let’s do that. I’d rather not make a big change just before I go away[/5].

dialogue.speaker: hairdresser
dialogue.text: How would you like your hair cut today? The same length as last time?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’m going on holiday somewhere hot, so I was thinking of having it much shorter. But I’m not sure that would suit me.

dialogue.speaker: hairdresser
dialogue.text: We could take a little off today and see how you feel.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, let’s do that. I’d rather not make a big change just before I go away.

questions.question: What does the customer decide to do?

options.C: wait until after her holiday for a haircut

questions.explanation: She agrees to have only a little cut off rather than make a big change before her holiday (evidence [5]).

extracts.context: You hear a student talking about a school play.
extracts.audio_script: Presenter: Did you enjoy being in the school play, Henry? ||Boy: Yes. I only had a small part, so learning my words wasn’t difficult. I thought I might forget them when the audience arrived, but that wasn’t a problem. [6]I hadn’t realised how much time we’d spend waiting during rehearsals while the lighting and scenery were prepared[/6]. That was the hard part.

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: Did you enjoy being in the school play, Henry?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Yes. I only had a small part, so learning my words wasn’t difficult. I thought I might forget them when the audience arrived, but that wasn’t a problem. I hadn’t realised how much time we’d spend waiting during rehearsals while the lighting and scenery were prepared. That was the hard part.

questions.question: What did Henry find difficult about taking part in the play?

options.B: performing in front of an audience

questions.explanation: Henry says the hard part was waiting while lighting and scenery were organised (evidence [6]).

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues discussing a training day.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Have you signed up for the training day about giving presentations? ||Man: I wanted to, but it’s on Friday, when I usually meet clients. ||Woman: The client meetings have been moved to Thursday this week. ||Man: Really? [7]Then I’ll register for the training; I need to become more confident when I speak at meetings[/7]. Is there still time? ||Woman: Yes, you can sign up today.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you signed up for the training day about giving presentations?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I wanted to, but it’s on Friday, when I usually meet clients.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: The client meetings have been moved to Thursday this week.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Really? Then I’ll register for the training; I need to become more confident when I speak at meetings. Is there still time?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, you can sign up today.

questions.question: Why does the man decide to attend the training day?

options.A: He no longer has another commitment that day.
options.B: His manager has told him he must attend.
options.C: He wants to meet new clients there.

questions.explanation: He decides to register after learning his client meetings will no longer clash with the training (evidence [7]).

extracts.context: You hear a presenter talking about a new café.
extracts.audio_script: Presenter: The Corner Café has reopened after being expanded, with additional tables upstairs and an area for young children. From next month it will also open on Sunday mornings. ||Presenter: [8]During this first week, anyone ordering a hot drink after two o’clock will receive a piece of homemade cake without paying extra[/8]. The café is opposite the post office and is open until six.

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: The Corner Café has reopened after being expanded, with additional tables upstairs and an area for young children. From next month it will also open on Sunday mornings.

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: During this first week, anyone ordering a hot drink after two o’clock will receive a piece of homemade cake without paying extra. The café is opposite the post office and is open until six.

questions.question: What is the café offering during its first week?

options.A: free cake with certain drink orders
options.B: free drinks for families with children
options.C: Sunday opening at no extra cost

questions.explanation: The presenter says afternoon customers ordering a hot drink will get cake at no additional charge (evidence [8]).
